diagrams.title: Environment Classification Taxonomy
diagrams.description: A hierarchical diagram showing the classification of environments based on key properties: Observability (Fully/Partially), Determinism (Deterministic/Stochastic), Dynamics (Static/Dynamic), State Space (Discrete/Continuous), Agents (Single/Multi), and Nature (Benign/Adversarial).
diagrams.purpose: To visualize all the dimensions along which environments can be classified, helping students understand the full landscape of environment types in AI.

diagrams.title: PEAS Framework Diagram
diagrams.description: A diagram showing the PEAS (Performance measure, Environment, Actuators, Sensors) components and their relationships to the Agent and Environment.
diagrams.purpose: To illustrate the interaction between an agent and its environment through the PEAS framework, showing how information flows and actions are executed.

diagrams.title: Agent-Environment Interaction Loop
diagrams.description: A cycle diagram showing the continuous interaction loop between an intelligent agent and its environment: Sensors perceive → Agent processes → Actuators act → Environment changes → Repeat.
elements: Feedback loop arrow back to Environment
diagrams.purpose: To visualize the ongoing cycle of perception, decision, and action that defines how agents interact with their environments.
